task_completion.description: The agent accesses the specified packet capture file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show Wireshark opening '/home/ga/Documents/captures/smtp.pcap' or a terminal executing a command (like tshark or tcpflow) on this file.

task_completion.description: The agent identifies and isolates the SMTP conversation within the packet capture.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wireshark shows a display filter for 'smtp' or 'tcp.port==25', or the agent selects an SMTP packet (e.g., containing EHLO or MAIL FROM) to follow the stream.

task_completion.description: The agent reconstructs and views the full TCP stream of the email conversation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Follow TCP Stream' dialog in Wireshark is open, or terminal output displays the plaintext SMTP conversation including the '220' greeting, 'EHLO', 'MAIL FROM', and 'RCPT TO' commands.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates saving the reconstructed TCP stream content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog is open in Wireshark's Follow TCP Stream window, or a shell command is redirecting the stream output to a file.

task_completion.description: The agent successfully saves the stream to the exact specified output path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (like 'ls', 'cat', or 'head') or a file manager shows that '/home/ga/Documents/captures/smtp_stream.txt' has been created and contains the SMTP conversation text.

integrity.description: The agent used appropriate network analysis tools to extract the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with Wireshark, tshark, or similar packet analysis tools rather than attempting to read the binary .pcap file with a standard text editor.

integrity.description: The extracted text matches the actual contents of the provided pcap file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visible stream content contains specific authentic details from the capture, such as 'MAIL FROM: <gurpartap@patriots.in>' or '220-xc90.websitewelcome.com', rather than generic or hallucinated SMTP examples.

integrity.description: The output file was generated by saving the stream, not manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses a 'Save' function from the analysis tool or terminal redirection (>), rather than opening a blank text editor and manually typing out an SMTP conversation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The smtp.pcap file contains 60 packets with 32 SMTP frames. The correct TCP stream contains an SMTP conversation with the greeting '220-xc90.websitewelcome.com ESMTP', 'EHLO', 'MAIL FROM: <gurpartap@patriots.in>', and 'RCPT TO: <raj_deol2002in@yahoo.co.in>'. The output must be saved exactly to /home/ga/Documents/captures/smtp_stream.txt.
